critic_raw:
CRITIQUE — real_world_framing_008 (Run 2)

1. CAH OUT-OF-DISTRIBUTION — NO VALIDATION: Validation at academic medical centers does not generalize to critical access hospitals. Different patient populations, acuity, staffing, and workflows. No CAH data in validation set.

2. ALERT FATIGUE CONTEXT IGNORED: >60% of existing alerts dismissed without action at these specific CAHs. Adding a high-sensitivity sepsis alert into a documented high-alert-fatigue environment will predictably result in sepsis alerts being dismissed with existing noise.

3. CAH WORKFLOW ACTIONABILITY GAP: CAHs lack intensivists, nurses manage 4–6 patients. Clinical response to a sepsis alert (sepsis bundle: lactate, cultures, antibiotics) may not be executable with existing CAH staffing. Unactionable alert creates liability without clinical benefit.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE — real_world_framing_008 (Run 2)

All three valid. CONCEDE on all three. Network-wide deployment including CAHs is inappropriate without CAH-specific validation, alert fatigue assessment, and workflow feasibility study.
adjudication_raw: All three issues conceded. Verdict: critique_wins — network-wide CAH deployment inappropriate without specific validation.
